skill.name: mixpanel-automation
skill.description: Automates Mixpanel analytics workflows through Rube MCP, including events, segmentation, funnels, cohorts, profiles, and JQL queries.
skill.summary: Use Rube MCP to run Mixpanel analytics queries and profile workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many external command and blocker patterns, but review found these are Markdown backticks, tool names, and plain documentation examples rather than executable code. The skill does require a network MCP endpoint and authenticated Mixpanel access, including read queries, batch profile updates, and JQL execution, so it should be published with a clear integration risk warning.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Mixpanel Operations Through External MCP
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to add the Rube MCP endpoint and connect an active Mixpanel account. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it routes analytics workflows through an external MCP integration and can access project, event, funnel, cohort, and profile data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation explicitly requires Rube MCP and an active Mixpanel connection. This confirms third-party network integration, but no malicious destination or hidden exfiltration is present.

medium_findings.title: Batch Profile Updates Can Modify Analytics Data
medium_findings.description: The skill documents Mixpanel profile batch updates with set, unset, add, and append operations. This is an intended capability, but incorrect prompts or broad filters could change user profile data at scale.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The profile update workflow is clearly documented and includes mutation operations. The risk depends on user authorization and prompt scope, so it is operational rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: Custom JQL Execution Requires Review
medium_findings.description: The skill supports submitting custom JQL scripts to Mixpanel. This is a legitimate analytics feature, but generated scripts should be reviewed because they execute against production analytics data and may be slow or overly broad.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The JQL workflow is explicitly described as custom JavaScript-like query execution. It is bounded to Mixpanel tooling, but generated query logic can still affect data exposure and service limits.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The many Ruby or shell backtick alerts correspond to Markdown inline code and fenced examples naming MCP tools, Mixpanel parameters, and expression syntax. No evidence found of executable shell commands, Ruby code, or command injection behavior in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows Markdown documentation using backticks for tool names and parameters. There is no script file or executable instruction that runs those strings as shell commands.

low_findings.title: Static Blocker Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and network reconnaissance alerts match ordinary words such as id, query, and read access in Mixpanel documentation. No evidence found of cryptographic code, host reconnaissance, port scanning, or local system enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are descriptive analytics instructions and table entries. They do not contain cryptographic functions, reconnaissance commands, or local system access.

dangerous_patterns.title: External MCP Endpoint Configuration
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs users to configure https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server. This is an expected integration path, but users should understand it involves a third-party service connection.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint is hardcoded in setup instructions and is central to the integration. It is not hidden or suspicious, but it is a real network dependency.

dangerous_patterns.title: Mixpanel Profile Mutation Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes a profile batch update workflow that can mutate Mixpanel user profile properties. This should require explicit user confirmation before broad changes.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documented batch update operation includes property mutation actions. The pattern is not malicious, but it is potentially destructive if used incorrectly.

content.value_statement: Mixpanel workflows often require exact tool schemas, IDs, dates, and filter syntax. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Rube MCP workflows for analytics queries and profile operations.
actual_capabilities: Guides setup for Rube MCP and Mixpanel account connection.
Lists Mixpanel projects before project-specific analytics workflows.
Runs event aggregation and segmentation workflows with date and property filters.
Queries saved funnels, cohorts, insights, and profile records.
Documents profile batch update operations and pagination handling.
Provides Mixpanel expression syntax patterns for filters and breakdowns.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP and an active Mixpanel connection.
Depends on current Rube tool schemas returned at runtime.
Cannot create Mixpanel funnels or cohorts from the documented workflows.
Large queries may hit Mixpanel rate limits or execution time limits.

use_cases.title: Review Product Adoption
use_cases.description: Analyze key events over time and break results down by plan, country, or device properties.

use_cases.title: Diagnose Funnel Drop-Off
use_cases.description: Find saved funnels, run conversion analysis, and segment drop-off by relevant user attributes.

use_cases.title: Maintain User Profiles
use_cases.description: Search profile records, page through results, and prepare controlled batch updates for selected users.

prompt_templates.title: List Available Projects
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Mixpanel automation skill. Search Rube tools first, confirm my Mixpanel connection, list available projects, and summarize each project ID.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Event Counts
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Mixpanel through Rube MCP to aggregate the Signup Completed event by day for the last 30 days. Confirm the exact date range before querying.

prompt_templates.title: Segment a Funnel
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the onboarding funnel, run it for the last full calendar month, and segment results by plan. Explain the main drop-off points.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Profile Updates
prompt_templates.prompt: Query profiles where plan is trial and last login is older than 60 days. Prepare a batch update plan, but ask for confirmation before changing any profiles.

output_examples.input: Show daily purchase events for the past week.
output_examples.output: A concise trend summary with the project used, date range, daily totals, and any missing or unusually low days.

output_examples.input: Find where users leave the checkout funnel.
output_examples.output: A funnel report naming each step, conversion rate, drop-off count, and likely segment differences.

output_examples.input: Update trial users with a lifecycle stage property.
output_examples.output: A confirmation-ready update plan with filter criteria, affected profile estimate, proposed property change, and rollback notes.

best_practices: Search Rube tool schemas before every workflow because parameters may change.
Use narrow date ranges and explicit filters before expanding analysis scope.
Require human confirmation before batch profile updates or broad JQL queries.
anti_patterns: Do not assume project, funnel, cohort, or insight IDs from names alone.
Do not run broad profile updates without a preview of affected records.
Do not use high-cardinality segmentation without a limit or smaller date range.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Mixpanel API key?
faq.answer: It requires an active Mixpanel connection through Rube MCP. Authentication is handled through the MCP connection flow.

faq.question: Can it create new funnels in Mixpanel?
faq.answer: No. The documented workflow lists and queries saved funnels that already exist in Mixpanel.

faq.question: Can it change user profile data?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents batch profile updates, so changes should be reviewed and confirmed before execution.

faq.question: Why does it search tools first?
faq.answer: Rube tool schemas may change. Searching first helps the assistant use current parameter names and requirements.

faq.question: Does it support custom Mixpanel queries?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes JQL and saved insight workflows when those features are available for the project.

faq.question: What should users check before running large queries?
faq.answer: They should confirm the project, date range, filters, segmentation limits, and expected data volume.
